title: Parking pawl module
abstract: A parking pawl module (10) includes a first actuator (100), a gearbox (200) including at least one gear carrier (220) where the at least one gear carrier supports a hub (260) configured to rotate when the first actuator rotates, and a disconnect module (400). The disconnect module includes: an output coupler (410) having a first surface (412) and a second surface (414); an actuation feature (240, 240c) disposed on the first surface; and a torsion spring (450) having a first end (452) connected to the output coupler and a second end (453) connected to the hub or the at least one gear carrier. The disconnect module also includes an actuation mechanism (430) disposed on the second surface of the output coupler. The actuation mechanism is configured to mesh with the hub. The first actuator controls movement of the at least one hub, the actuation feature, and the actuation mechanism.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A parking pawl module (10) comprising: a first actuator (100); a gearbox (200) comprising at least one gear carrier (220), the at least one gear carrier (220) supporting a hub (260) configured to rotate when the first actuator (100) rotates; a disconnect module (400) comprising: an output coupler (410) having a first surface (412) and a second surface (414); an actuation feature (240, 240c) disposed on the first surface (412) of the output coupler (410); a torsion spring (450) having a first end (452) connected to the output coupler (410) and a second end (453) connected to the hub (260) or the at least one gear carrier (220); and an actuation mechanism (430) disposed on the second surface (414) of the output coupler (410) and extending away from the output coupler (410), the actuation mechanism (430) configured to mesh with the hub (260) of the gearbox (200); wherein the first actuator (100) controls movement of the at least one hub (260), the actuation feature (240, 240c), and the actuation mechanism (430). 2. The parking pawl module (10) of claim 1, further comprising: a toggle lever (310) defining a lever aperture (312) configured to receive the actuation feature (240, 240c) and allow movement of the actuation feature (240, 240c) within the lever aperture (312); a shaft (314) attached to the toggle lever (310), wherein movement of the actuation feature (240, 240c) causes movement of the shaft (314). 3. The parking pawl module (10) of claim 2, wherein movement of the actuation feature (240, 240c) in an arcuate motion along an arcuate path (242) causes a linear 

 motion of the shaft (314) about a chordal path (320) of an arc (242) defining the arcuate motion. 4. The parking pawl module (10) of claim 1, wherein the output coupler (410) includes at least one flange (416) extending away from a center of the output coupler (410), the at least one flange (416) comprises first and second edges (419), wherein the first and second edges (419) define at least one valley (417) therebetween. 5. The parking pawl module (10) of claim 4, further comprising a cam coupler (460) comprising: a belt mount step (462) having a first belt mount step side (462a) and a second belt mount step side (462b); a first step (464) extending away from the first belt mount step side (462a) and having a diameter that is less than a diameter of the belt mount step (462); a second step (466) extending away from the second belt mount step side (462b), the second step (466) including a step surface (467) on a distal end of the second step (466)not connected to the belt mount step (462); at least one cam (470) extending away from the step surface (467), the at least one cam (470) configured to mesh with the at least one valley (417) defined by the first and second edges (419). 6. The parking pawl module (10) of claim 5, further comprising an auxiliary motor connected to the cam coupler (460) by way of a belt, the auxiliary motor configured to rotate the cam coupler (460) with respect to the output coupler (410), wherein rotation of the cam coupler (460) causes one of the first and/or second edge (419) of the output coupler (410) to climb over the at least one cam (470) of the cam coupler (460), which causes the output coupler (410) to shift axially away from the cam coupler (460). 7. The parking pawl module (10) of claim 6, wherein when the output coupler (410) shifts axially away from the cam coupler (460), the torsion spring (450) causes the output coupler (410) to rotate in a direction that triggers a park configuration of a vehicle. 8. A parking pawl module (10) comprising: a first sun gear (230a); a first actuator (100) configured to actuate the first sun gear (230a); a gearbox (200) comprising: a gearbox housing (210); at least one gear carrier (220, 220a, 220b) housed by the gearbox housing (210), the at least one gear carrier (220, 220a, 220b) having a first carrier side (221a) and a second carrier side (221b); at least one plurality of planetary gears (222, 222a, 222b) supported on the first carrier side (221a) of the at least one gear carrier (220, 220a, 220b), the at least one plurality of planetary gears (222, 222a, 222b) is in mesh with the first sun gear (230a); and an actuation feature (240, 240c) connected to the gearbox (200); a toggle lever (310) defining a lever aperture (254) configured to receive the actuation feature (240, 240c) and allow movement of the actuation feature (240, 240c) within the lever aperture (254); and a shaft (314) attached to the toggle lever (310); wherein the first actuator (100) controls a rotational movement of the at least one gear carrier (220, 220a, 220b), the plurality of planetary gears (222, 222a, 222b), and the actuation feature (240, 240c), and a linear motion of the toggle lever (310) and the shaft (314). 9. The parking pawl module (10) of claim 8, wherein the actuation feature (240, 240c) is an actuation pin. 10. The parking pawl module (10) of claim 8, wherein the parking pawl module (10) is connected to a parking pawl, wherein a movement of the actuation feature (240, 240c) in a first direction triggers the parking pawl to be in a park configuration and a movement of the actuation feature (240, 240c) in a second direction opposite the first direction triggers the parking pawl to be out of park configuration. 11. The parking pawl module (10) of claim 8, wherein the at least one gear carrier (220, 220a, 220b) comprises: a first gear carrier (220a) having a first carrier side (221a) and a second carrier side (221b); and a second gear carrier (220b) having a first carrier side (221a) and a second carrier side (221b), the second gear carrier (220b) comprising a second sun gear (230b) positioned on the first carrier side (221a). 12. The parking pawl module (10) of claim 11, wherein the at least one plurality of planetary gears (222, 222a, 222b) comprises: a first plurality of planetary gears (222a) supported on the first carrier side (221a) of the first gear carrier (220a), the first plurality of planetary gears (222a) is in mesh with the first sun gear (230a); and a second plurality of planetary gears (222b) supported on the first carrier side (221a) of the second gear carrier (220b), the second plurality of planetary gears (222b) is in mesh with the second sun gear (230b). 13. The parking pawl module (10) of claim 12, wherein the actuation feature (240, 240c) is connected to the second carrier side (221b) of the second gear carrier (220b). 14. The parking pawl module (10) of claim 13, wherein an arcuate motion of the actuation feature (240, 240c) causes a linear motion of the shaft (314) about a chordal path (320) of an arc (242) defining the arcuate motion. 15. The parking pawl module (10) of claim 1, further comprising a ring gear (212) integrally formed on an inner side of the gearbox housing (210), the ring gear (212) is in mesh with the at least one plurality of planetary gears (222, 222a, 222b).
